Get Started — Develop

Build Categorization Process

A structured sub-process of the SDLC that manages software releases, controls quality, and establishes a clear standard for build classification.

Overview

SDLC Issues Addressed

  • Build credibility issue
  • Quality assurance issue
  • Prioritization challenges
  • Standards

Goals

  • Manage the software development lifecycle efficiently
  • Control the quality of releases
  • A clear understanding of the software's stability and features at different stages of development
  • Process adaptability in the long run

SDLC Integration

The Build Categorization Process is a sub-process of the NESV SDLC process. It is realized during the Business Analysis phase, specifically within the Validation (Requirements Review) stage.

1 Business Analysis Active Phase
2 Development
3 Deployment
4 Support
Business Analysis Phase Breakdown
1
Elicitation
2
Analysis
3
Specification
4
Validation (Requirements Review) Build Categorization Process

Process Steps

Standard Change Request

a

Finalize HLS Document

  • Head should list and finalize the scope in the HLS document.
  • In the event Head is not available, Senior SA should take over.
b

Arrange Discussion with Technical Team for Build Categorization

c

Cost Estimate Creation

d

Approval from Management

e

Sales Quotation Creation

Ad-hoc Change Request

a

Create Task

  • Tag CL version as TBD
Format v[TBD] Change Request – Project Title
b

Arrange Discussion with Technical Team for Build Categorization

  • In the event Heads are not available for discussion, Senior Devs should take over.

Build Types

Major Build

Build Requirements

  • New Project
  • Framework-related encompassing multiple modules
  • Major Integration
    • API
Minor Build

Build Requirements — One (1) or more significant features

  • Minor Integration
    • API
  • Additional Module (Menu)
  • Additional Pages
    • Reports
  • New Jobs
  • New email features
  • Major Schema Changes
  • UI Modifications
    • Mobile responsive
Release Build

Build Requirements — 1–10 minor features

  • CRUD
  • Filters
  • UI Modifications
  • Minor Schema Changes
    • 1–3 columns
  • Additional Pages
    • Minor Functionality
  • Performance Enhancement
Build or Hotfix

Build Requirements

  • Hotfix / Bug fix
    • CL related
      • Showstopper
      • Destructive for User Experience (UI / Data Issue)
  • Release
    • Non-CL related
      • Destructive for User Experience (UI / Data Issue)
On this page
